(2) The court officer will serve notice of the hearing referred to at paragraph (1)(b) on the parties to the proceedings.


(3) The court must, in writing—


(4) The court may with the consent of all the parties deal with any question as to the jurisdiction of the court, or as to whether the proceedings should be stayed (GL) , without a hearing.


Textual Amendments


F122 Words in rule 12.68(1) omitted (31.12.2020) by virtue of The Family Procedure Rules 2010 and Court of Protection Rules 2017 (Amendment) (EU Exit) Regulations 2019 (S.I. 2019/517) , regs. 1(1) , 9(17) (with reg. 23 ) (as amended by S.I. 2020/1493 , regs. 1(1) , 8(2)(b) ); 2020 c. 1 , Sch. 5 para. 1(1)


#### Request for consultation as to contemplated placement of child in England and Wales E+W


12.69. —(1) This rule applies to a request made —


for consultation on or consent to the contemplated placement of a child in England and Wales.


(2) Where the court receives a request directly from a court in another F124 ... Contracting State, the court shall, as soon as practicable after receipt of the request, notify the [ F125 domestic Central Authority ] of the request and take the appropriate action under paragraph (4).


(3) Where it appears to the court officer that no proceedings relating to the child are pending before a court in England and Wales, the court officer must inform the [ F125 domestic Central Authority ] of that fact and forward to the Central Authority all documents relating to the request sent by the court in the other F126 ... Contracting State.


(4) Where the court receives a request forwarded by the [ F125 domestic Central Authority ] , the court must, as soon as practicable after receipt of the request, either—
